and circumstances of the case, we find that the appellant succeeded 
in establishing his case and the Orders passed by the Trial Court and 
the Appellate Court did not warrant any interference. The High Court 
erred in overturning the concurrent findings of guilt and consequential 
conviction by the Trial Court and the Appellate Court.
24. Accordingly, for reasons aforesaid, the appeal is allowed. The 
Impugned Order is set aside. Though the natural consequence 
would entail revival of the conviction and sentence imposed upon 
the respondent no.2 i.e., one year simple imprisonment and fine of 
Rs.35,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty-Five Lakhs), but having regard to 
the parting submissions of learned senior counsel for the accused/
respondent no.2, to the effect that considering his age, he may be 
520 
only subjected to fine and not imprisonment, we are inclined to modify 
the sentence to only payment of a fine restricted to Rs.32,00,000/-
